run_summary: Screen 1 holds 7 open positions (2 DOCN duplicates) averaging 13 days into the fade window; 3 are near or past recovery targets, 1 showing gains. Four new flags on the Claude Cowork expansion trigger—three are basket-rule EXPLORATORY (ONTO, AEIS, PLAB all hit at days 5-13 already held, all fresh overlaps with existing positions), one weaker indirect threat (CGNX) stays WATCH.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
entries.thesis: A coding-agent product from Anthropic has zero mechanical bearing on demand for advanced-node process control equipment. If anything, more AI chips (which is what Cowork-style demand ultimately drives) means more wafer starts at leading nodes, which means MORE metrology/inspection tools. The five-day drawdown makes this cheaper than a clean sympathy-only setup, but there's no filings-based reason to think the trigger event actually impairs the thesis.
entries.what_kills: A leading-edge fab capex cut announcement, a WFE downgrade cycle from Applied/LAM/KLAC, or an ONTO-specific customer-loss 8-K within the fade window.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: ONTO already held (5 days, -19.82% unrealized). Fresh flag re-confirms thesis on Claude Cowork trigger: threat_assessment='none' (metrology/inspection has zero mechanical link to coding agents), panic_calibration='unjustified' with crisp filings evidence (Risk Factors cite AI as internal tool only, not competitive threat). Confidence 4. However, ONTO is part of a 3-name basket (ONTO, AEIS, PLAB) all flagged on same Claude Cowork catalyst—basket rule applies, forcing EXPLORATORY tier despite strong individual merits. Sized 6% exploratory, not 20% conviction. Fresh drawdown (-7.88% 1-day, -20.35% 5-day) provides technical entry opportunity within the fade window.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis assumes the five-day -20.35% drawdown is irrational sympathy fade, but doesn't rule out that broader semi-cap capex anxiety (unrelated to Claude Cowork) is the true driver and may persist.
red_team.critique: The bull case rests on the premise that metrology/inspection tools have zero mechanical link to a coding-agent product release, which is sound. However, the -20.35% five-day move and -7.88% single-day move occur in a market context where WFE sentiment can shift rapidly on any macro signal. The flag acknowledges 'no company-specific bad news' but doesn't address whether the drawdown itself signals broader fab capex hesitation that ONTO filings haven't yet captured. The bull thesis treats this as a pure sympathy fade, but the data allows for a genuine capex-cycle inflection that the filings haven't disclosed yet.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
entries.catalyst: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
entries.thesis: AEIS's Risk Factors flag AI as a demand TAILWIND, not a threat. A Claude coding-agent product doesn't compete with power conversion for fabs or data centers — the opposite: agentic workloads driving inference demand require more of exactly what AEIS sells. The five-day drawdown has made this cheaper still.
entries.what_kills: A specific AEIS customer (Applied Materials, LAM) issues a capex cut, a large data-center hyperscaler pauses buildout, or an AEIS 8-K reveals an operational issue.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: AEIS already held (13 days, -21.5% unrealized). Fresh flag confirms thesis on Claude Cowork: threat_assessment='none', panic_calibration='unjustified' with explicit 10-K evidence (AI infrastructure investments are demand TAILWIND for power delivery). Confidence 4. Part of 3-name Claude Cowork basket (ONTO, AEIS, PLAB)—basket rule overrides individual conviction merit. Exploratory tier, 6% sizing. Near end of 15-day window; addition is a final reinforcement before close.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis depends on power-delivery demand remaining robust despite the broader tech selloff, but volume_multiple of 0.7 (below average) suggests weak accumulation into the drawdown and may indicate institutional hesitation rather than pure sympathy.
red_team.critique: The mechanical logic—that AI workloads require more power conversion—is solid and supported by the filings interpretation. However, the volume_multiple of 0.7 is a material red flag: if this were a genuine sympathy-driven mispricing, one would expect elevated volume on the way down as smart money accumulated. Instead, below-average volume combined with a -17.35% five-day drawdown suggests this may be a liquidity-driven flush or a signal that the market is discounting something beyond the catalyst alone. The bull case doesn't account for why institutional buyers are NOT showing up proportionally on this dip.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
entries.thesis: Every chip that exists — including every accelerator that runs Claude Cowork inference — requires photomasks. There's no mechanism by which an application-layer AI agent reduces mask demand; it increases it. Panic on this news is mechanically backwards. Curation hypothesis confirmed by filings.
entries.what_kills: A specific customer-concentration event (top-2 customers are 29% of revenue) — if one of them cuts orders. Or an escalation of China export controls hitting Photronics's two China fabs directly.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: PLAB already held (13 days, -13.35% unrealized). Fresh flag confirms thesis on Claude Cowork: threat_assessment='none', panic_calibration='unjustified' (photomasks required for all accelerator chips, no filings mention of coding-agent threat). Confidence 4. Part of 3-name Claude Cowork basket (ONTO, AEIS, PLAB)—basket rule forces exploratory tier despite strong individual metrics. 6% sizing. Final-window add.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis correctly identifies photomasks as required for all chips, but the 'design release' driver of mask demand is not quantified or timed—the bull case doesn't establish when these design releases are actually expected to occur or whether current drawdown reflects a timing/execution risk.
red_team.critique: The photomask argument is mechanically sound: more accelerators mean more mask orders. However, the flag cites 'IC design activity metrics remain strong' as a confirmer without naming which metrics, when they'll inflect, or what lag exists between design release and revenue recognition. The -11.92% five-day move is modest relative to the basket peers, which could imply PLAB has already been repriced, or could mean the market is unconvinced of the timing thesis. The bull case assumes design activity will translate to near-term mask orders, but doesn't address the rhythm or the risk that the AI chip cycle has already moved past peak design announcements.

entries.name: Cognex Corporation
entries.catalyst: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
entries.thesis: Cognex has real, filings-acknowledged AI competition — but it's from open-source machine-vision models on factory floors, NOT from a coding agent for office productivity. Claude Cowork does not do deterministic sub-millisecond machine vision on a QA line. The panic is thematic, applying a general 'AI eats software' narrative to a very specific factory-floor workflow. The filings acknowledge the general threat but the trigger event itself does nothing to accelerate it.
entries.what_kills: A major factory-automation customer (auto OEM, EV battery, life sciences) publicly switching to a general-purpose AI vision solution; or an open-source model demonstrating deterministic latency parity in an industrial-grade setting.
entries.reasoning: Cognex flags threat_assessment='indirect' (filings acknowledge general AI competition in machine vision, but from open-source models, not from Claude Cowork coding agents). Panic_calibration='unjustified' (trigger event is office productivity agent, not factory-floor vision). Confidence 3 (at min threshold). Filings evidence is thinner—threat is real but orthogonal to this specific catalyst. Industrial machine vision QA is not addressed by Claude Cowork. Borderline case: meets BUY eligibility on raw criteria (OVERDONE, unjustified, conf 3) but threat_assessment='indirect' and catalyst link is weak. WATCH to gather more data on whether Cognex customers actually adopt Claude agents for workflow optimization—if so, thesis may strengthen for a later entry.
